bull: Preliminary Q4 update shows record $60B+ order backlog and gross margins well above forecast, sparking a 27% intraday surge on 4.3x volume.
Deep -51% drawdown from 52w high leaves room to re-rate if the margin beat proves durable.
bear: Still -6.9% below SMA50 and -9.3% below SMA200 despite the spike, so the downtrend isn't technically broken yet.
SMCI's history of accounting/execution issues makes a single preliminary guidance jump risky to chase.
SMCI.summary: Supermicro surged 20-27% on a record order backlog and margin beat, but the move needs to hold above its moving averages to confirm a real trend reversal.
SMCI.key_risk: Given SMCI's checkered accounting history, today's guidance beat could prove unreliable and the stock could round-trip the gain.

bull: Raised annual subscription revenue guidance for the second time on AI-driven demand despite the post-earnings drop.
RSI 34 / stoch 3.8 signal a stretched oversold condition after the selloff.
bear: Stock remains -25% below SMA200 in a confirmed downtrend, and the light near-term guidance is what triggered the selloff.
NOW.summary: ServiceNow beat Q2 estimates and raised its AI-driven subscription outlook, but light near-term guidance drove a sharp drop that leaves it deeply oversold inside an intact downtrend.
NOW.key_risk: If AI-driven demand doesn't convert into accelerating billings, further multiple compression is likely given still-elevated valuation expectations.

bull: Deeply oversold (stoch 0.1, RSI 33) after a -15.5% one-month slide, with recurring revenue and the da Vinci 5/Ion pipeline keeping the long-term growth case intact.
bear: Analysts are actively cutting fair-value estimates as US procedure growth slows and tariffs pressure margins — a fundamental, not just technical, reset.
ISRG.summary: Intuitive Surgical is being punished on slowing procedure growth even as its device pipeline stays strong, leaving it deeply oversold but still in a confirmed downtrend.
ISRG.key_risk: Further multiple compression if revenue growth keeps decelerating toward the lower estimates analysts are now pricing in.

bull: New growth optionality in application lifecycle management (Codebeamer) as software-defined products drive regulatory-linked ALM demand.
Stoch 7.4 shows a short-term oversold bounce setup.
bear: Broad downtrend intact at -27.8% below SMA200 despite the ALM narrative, and the stock just gave back another 6%+ in a single day.
PTC.summary: PTC's Codebeamer/ALM growth story hasn't stopped the stock from sliding further into a deep, oversold downtrend.
PTC.key_risk: If the ALM narrative fails to show up in bookings growth, the stock likely continues grinding toward new lows alongside enterprise software peers.

bull: Blew out Q2 estimates with 17.5% revenue growth and raised full-year guidance on strong backlog and international demand.
Confirmed by a record-high breakout on 3.4x average volume, only -1.8% from its 52-week high.
bear: RSI 74 and price above the upper Bollinger Band signal a stretched, overbought move vulnerable to near-term profit-taking.
WAB.summary: Wabtec beat Q2 estimates and raised guidance on strong backlog growth, driving shares to a record high on heavy volume, though the move is now technically overbought.
WAB.key_risk: A short-term pullback from overbought technical levels is likely even if the fundamental guidance raise holds up.

bull: Service-segment organic growth is accelerating and offsetting soft New Equipment demand, with Q2 EPS and revenue both beating estimates.
bear: Management trimmed part of its profit outlook to fund service-quality and retention investments, capping near-term margin upside.
OTIS.summary: Otis beat Q2 estimates on strong Service growth but lowered part of its profit outlook to fund retention investments, leaving the technical picture mixed.
OTIS.key_risk: Continued weakness in the New Equipment business could outpace Service growth if elevator/escalator capex stays soft.

bull: RSI 20.5 is deeply oversold, and Mizuho is explicitly recommending buying the EDA-sector dip, arguing new AI model releases reinforce rather than threaten the EDA thesis.
bear: The stock keeps falling despite a 'beneficiary of AI regardless of winner' framing, suggesting the market is pricing in a risk not yet reflected in headline numbers.
SNPS.summary: Synopsys is deeply oversold after a steep three-week slide, and at least one desk is calling it a buying opportunity even though the reason for the drop isn't fully explained by the numbers.
SNPS.key_risk: If the selloff reflects a real competitive threat to EDA rather than sector-wide de-rating, the oversold bounce thesis could fail to play out.

bull: RSI 17.6 is a textbook extreme-oversold reading, and analysts have been raising earnings estimates into the August 5 print, suggesting sentiment is troughing.
bear: The stock is down nearly 22% in just 20 days, showing the lithium-price downcycle is still repricing the stock faster than estimate revisions can offset.
ALB.summary: Albemarle is extremely oversold heading into its August 5 earnings even as analysts lift estimates, but the lithium downcycle keeps outpacing the improving fundamentals.
ALB.key_risk: A soft August 5 earnings print or continued lithium price weakness would extend the downtrend despite the oversold setup.

bull: Shares rose 3% on the actual Q2 miss because last week's profit warning had already reset expectations, and stoch 1.2 signals capitulation-level oversold conditions.
bear: IBM cut revenue guidance and posted its worst single-day cap wipeout since 1987 last week, confirming genuine fundamental deterioration, not just sentiment.
IBM.summary: IBM's Q2 miss was already priced in by last week's warning, leaving shares oversold, but the underlying guidance cut points to a real slowdown rather than just a sentiment reset.
IBM.key_risk: If the guidance cut signals a deeper slowdown in the software/consulting business, the stock could keep grinding lower even after the sentiment reset.

bull: Consensus price targets imply 35% upside, and stoch at 3.1 shows an extreme short-term oversold bounce setup heading into next week's earnings.
bear: Zacks flags weak beat-probability signals ahead of the print, and the stock remains -25.6% below its SMA200 in a still-intact downtrend.
TYL.summary: Tyler Technologies is deeply oversold heading into next week's earnings with a wide analyst-target gap, but beat-probability signals are unfavorable and the downtrend is intact.
TYL.key_risk: A weak earnings beat probability heading into next week's report could trigger another leg down if results disappoint.

bull: Trading right at both its 50- and 200-day moving averages with low volatility, giving a stable utility base as a scout candidate ahead of earnings.
bear: Simply Wall St flags the stock as roughly fairly valued already, and volume is running at just half of normal (0.5x), signaling limited conviction either way.
PEG.summary: PEG is a stable, roughly fairly-valued utility scout sitting right on its moving averages with low volume, offering no clear near-term catalyst.
PEG.key_risk: As a low-beta utility scout name, PEG offers limited upside catalyst unless storm-recovery capex or rate-case outcomes surprise positively.

bull: Uptrend intact near 52-week highs (+18.5% above SMA200) heading into new Mac launches aimed at AI demand.
bear: RSI 76 is firmly overbought, and commentary is flagging valuation risk plus an active OpenAI IP lawsuit exposing AI-strategy uncertainty.
AAPL.summary: Apple remains in a strong uptrend near record highs on its AI-hardware refresh, but RSI is overbought and an OpenAI IP lawsuit adds a fresh uncertainty.
AAPL.key_risk: A stretched RSI near all-time highs leaves the stock vulnerable to a pullback if AI-related headlines turn negative.

bull: Newly launched decentralized 'Buzz' platform expands Block's ecosystem beyond payments, and the stock is flagged on multiple momentum/earnings-beat watchlists.
bear: The stock pulled back -5.3% over 5 days even with bullish headline coverage, suggesting the market isn't yet rewarding the product diversification news.
XYZ.summary: Block launched a new decentralized social platform and shows up on momentum/earnings-beat screens, but the stock has sold off regardless over the past week.
XYZ.key_risk: If the new Buzz platform fails to gain traction or core Cash App/Square growth slows in upcoming earnings, the pullback could extend.

bull: Fifth consecutive earnings beat with raised full-year guidance and record margins, plus a newly disclosed AI/optical-networking tailwind via Microsoft Azure data centers.
bear: RSI 68 and price above the upper Bollinger Band show the stock is technically stretched near 52-week highs, and litigation risk still lingers.
MMM.summary: 3M keeps beating and raising, now with an AI-driven optical-networking tailwind, but the stock is technically stretched near record highs after the run.
MMM.key_risk: Lingering litigation overhang could resurface and cap the rerating even as operational execution keeps beating estimates.

bull: Wall Street remains bullish with price targets implying upside even after the stock fell 34% from its June peak, and the company keeps raising its full-year AI-bookings outlook.
bear: The stock is still down -24% over just 20 days despite accelerating bookings, showing the market is pricing in execution or competitive risk that fundamentals alone haven't resolved.
MRVL.summary: Marvell's AI bookings and guidance keep improving even as the stock keeps falling, leaving analysts split between a buying opportunity and a value trap.
MRVL.key_risk: If the market's skepticism reflects real competitive share loss to custom silicon rivals rather than just sentiment, the 'value trap' scenario could play out instead of a bounce.

bull: Nasdaq has a strong earnings-beat track record and reports Thursday with favorable beat-probability signals ahead of the print.
bear: Simply Wall St estimates the stock is already trading ~7% above fair value, limiting upside even if the beat materializes.
NDAQ.summary: Nasdaq heads into Thursday's earnings with a strong beat history, but the stock already looks modestly overvalued going in.
NDAQ.key_risk: A valuation already running ahead of fair-value estimates leaves little room for error if Thursday's earnings disappoint.

bull: Simply Wall St's DCF suggests the stock still trades below intrinsic value despite a strong 5-year run, supported by robust cash flow generation.
bear: RSI 70 and stoch 85 show overbought conditions heading into next week's earnings, and Zacks flags a weak beat-probability setup for the print.
NUE.summary: Nucor screens as undervalued on cash flow even after a strong run, but it's technically overbought and facing an unfavorable beat setup heading into next week's report.
NUE.key_risk: An overbought technical setup combined with unfavorable beat-probability signals raises the odds of a 'sell the news' reaction to next week's earnings.

bull: RSI 35 and stoch 20 show the stock is oversold after a steep -23.5% one-month slide, which could set up a technical bounce if AI-capex fears ease.
bear: Oracle's AI growth narrative is directly tied to the OpenAI/Stargate buildout, and reports that OpenAI can't afford its committed spend threaten the demand backbone behind Oracle's cloud capex bet.
ORCL.summary: Oracle's steep slide reflects growing doubts about whether its key AI partner, OpenAI, can actually afford the Stargate buildout that underpins Oracle's capex-heavy growth story.
ORCL.key_risk: If OpenAI scales back or delays its Stargate commitments, Oracle's aggressive AI-datacenter capex could turn into a significant earnings and cash-flow drag.

bull: Morgan Stanley remains constructive on aerospace/defense fundamentals, and stoch 1.6 signals an extreme oversold technical setup ahead of Q3 earnings.
bear: A top executive just cut his stake by 46%, an insider-selling signal that adds caution just as the stock enters its Q3 report.
TDG.summary: TransDigm is deeply oversold heading into Q3 earnings within a still-bullish aerospace sector, but notable insider selling tempers the setup.
TDG.key_risk: Notable insider selling ahead of earnings raises the risk that Q3 results or guidance disappoint relative to the resilient aerospace narrative.
